title: Etsy goes after Prime Day with ‘Shop Other Jeffs’ campaign
description: The marketplace's campaign features non-billionaire Etsy sellers named Jeff and includes limited-edition merchandise.

title: Takeaways on Google’s New AI Ad Features
description:
At its annual Marketing Live event last month, Google announced 70 new advertising features. 
The post Takeaways on Google’s New AI Ad Features appeared first on Practical Ecommerce.

title: Google’s ‘Non-Commodity’ Push Isn’t New
description:
Like EEAT before it, non-commodity content is not easily summarized or recycled by generative AI without attribution.
The post Google’s ‘Non-Commodity’ Push Isn’t New appeared first on Practical Ecommerce.

title: Faire opens its marketplace up to hotels, offices and non-retail customers
description: Faire is growing beyond boutiques by allowing business buyers to use the platform for their own purposes, like stocking mini bars, for client gifts or for front desk amenities.

title: How brands like Goldbelly, Chobani and Xochitl are showing up for America 250
description: Expect more limited-edition red, white and blue packaging on retail shelves this summer, as brands turn out to celebrate America 250. Brand leaders at Xochitl, Goldbelly and more spoke with Modern Retail about why they are turning out for the occasion and what purpose they think America 250-themed products serve.

title: New Ecommerce Tools: June 17, 2026
description:
This week's rundown includes new services for product images, affiliate sales, agentic commerce, bot protection, LTL shipping, product visibility, conversational search, and more.
The post New Ecommerce Tools: June 17, 2026 appeared first on Practical Ecommerce.

title: Adobe: AI-referred traffic to retail sites doubles in a year
description:
Traffic from artificial intelligence (AI) sources to U.S. ecommerce websites more than doubled year over year in May 2026, according to data from Adobe Analytics. It found that AI-referred traffic to retail sites grew 138% year over year in May 2026. In addition, AI-referred traffic to those sites has grown 1,324% (more than 14x growth) […]
The post Adobe: AI-referred traffic to retail sites doubles in a year appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: Advance Auto Parts expands partnership with OneRail on AI-powered fulfillment
description:
Advance Auto Parts announced an expanded partnership with OneRail on artificial intelligence (AI)-powered fulfillment orchestration. OneRail is an AI platform that helps retailers, wholesalers and distributors facilitate same-day delivery. Its expanded partnership with Advance Auto Parts is to help the retailer with investments in supply chain modernization, inventory availability, market hubs and store-based fulfillment, according […]
The post Advance Auto Parts expands partnership with OneRail on AI-powered fulfillment appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: commercetools debuts B2B Intake Agent that it developed with Mirion Technologies
description:
The ecommerce platform provider commercetools announced the release of the first artificial intelligence (AI) agent that it has co-developed with Mirion Technologies. The agent, which commercetools refers to as the B2B Intake Agent, is designed to help commercetools clients to take unstructured customer order requests and output actionable quotes and cart information. The launch follows […]
The post commercetools debuts B2B Intake Agent that it developed with Mirion Technologies appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: Edible Brands navigates AI traffic, strategy in part by replatforming
description:
Less than 10% of web traffic to Edible Brands’ websites comes from artificial intelligence (AI) platforms, according to Erica Randerson, chief digital officer of Edible Brands. Randerson told Digital Commerce 360 that Edible is looking at how its customers are finding the retailer. It’s also looking at how it shows up in more of these […]
The post Edible Brands navigates AI traffic, strategy in part by replatforming appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: Pinterest launches an experimental AI shopping app called ‘Ask Pinterest’
description: Pinterest has launched 'Ask Pinterest,' an experimental AI-powered shopping app that lets users seek recommendations and inspiration through a conversational interface.

title: Aleve tackles home improvement pain with social content and DIY stars
description: “Fixer to Fabulous” hosts Jenny and Dave Marrs star in a social media campaign from the Bayer pain relief brand.

title: P&G’s latest branded microdrama is built on Albertsons retail media data
description: The CPG giant is the first to use a new branded entertainment offering from the grocer’s retail media network that puts shopper insights first.

title: Hinge’s chief marketer on why community is more than a marketing channel
description: Chief Marketing and Communications Officer Tamika Young explains why the dating app has donated millions to grassroots social groups.

title: How the World Cup fuels US soccer leagues and their brand partners
description: The CMOs at Major League Soccer and the National Women's Soccer League explain how they’re marketing around the tournament.
